mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached over a 24-month evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in consumption and housing starts fails to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its 2017 baseline index of 100 during a tightening cycle, robust manufacturing output supports corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns do not materialize in >50% of weeks where industrial production remains above 100 during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 305 signal accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 305 threshold across any 36-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative or zero information coefficient over any 24-month rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following positive GDP growth releases, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over 5-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 102 alongside elevated inflation indicates stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and trigger risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The stagflation signal fails to predict negative returns with a win rate below 45% in out-of-sample backtests over any 3-year period.
